paper_title: Material and Methods of Celonites fischeri Study in Cyprus

methods.description: This method involves systematic field investigations to locate and document the presence of Celonites fischeri in the vicinity of Paphos, Cyprus, and to collect relevant environmental and biological data.

steps.description: Celonites fischeri was searched for systematically in the vicinity of Paphos by checking promising sites with large Echium populations.
steps.searchable_summary: search for Celonites fischeri in Echium-rich areas

steps.description: Geographic coordinates (WGS 84) were measured using a Garmin GPS 12.
Observations were made with a close-up binocular and documented by using a Canon EOS camera with a macro-lens and macro flash-lights.
steps.searchable_summary: document observations with binoculars and camera

steps.description: Specimens of all plant species flowering at locality II were collected and preserved dried.

methods.description: This method describes the preparation and analysis of pollen samples collected from Celonites fischeri to study their flower preferences and pollen types.

steps.description: Pollen samples from two brood cell provisions of nest F were prepared using the method outlined by Westrich and Schmidt (1986).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen samples using Westrich and Schmidt method

steps.description: Pooled pollen samples from four localities were prepared each with pollen from the crops of five females fixed in Duboscq-Brasil solution.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pooled pollen samples with Duboscq-Brasil solution

steps.description: The different pollen types were ascertained under a light microscope and determined to generic level with the aid of a reference collection.

methods.description: This method involves observing the behavior of Celonites fischeri and analyzing their nests to understand their spatial and temporal patterns.

steps.description: The behavior of a female at one of the nests was continuously investigated from 27 May until 30 May.
Spatial and temporal behavioral patterns were reconstructed by analysis of sequences of photographs.
The nests were marked in the field with little ice-cream national flags and named after the country code of the flag used.
Cell dimensions were measured using a strip of millimetre paper.
